free_text: Ph.D. March 2004
148Gd production cross section measurements for 600-
and 800-MeV protons on tantalum, tungsten, and gold
LANSCE accelerator and WNR Facility
Irradiation in the so-called Blue Room at WNR
Experiments were done with stack of foils and with
single foil, sandwiched between two aluminum catcher
The tantalum, tungsten, and gold foils (>99.9% purity)
were  nominally 3 mu-m in thickness so that proton
energy loss through  a foil was negligible.
Aluminum activation foils (10 mu-m thickness,
99.0% purity) were used for beam  monitoring
Monitor cross-sections were used to determine
the proton fluence by counting the 22Na with a HPGe
detector calibrated with a 152Eu source.
The gamma-spectroscopy system was used not only for
measuring 22Na, but also for measuring other
radionuclide production cross sections.
Uncertainty in proton flux
Uncertainty in areal density of target
DR

SD: MONITOR, MONIT-REF were added to
Subent 1. REACTION code was corrected in Subents
002-010. BIB updated in all Subents. Subents 5-10
were split (every Subent split in two) according
to independent or cumulative yields measured were from
radionuclides. Subents 11-16 added.
Total error includes: ERR-1; ERR-2 and
